# PlaceMate

> PlaceMate is a free, all-in-one web app for UK student nurses. It helps them track their placement (practice) hours, log their NMC proficiencies and clinical skills, plan shifts and revision, keep medication notes, practise drug calculations, and write structured reflections — all built around the Nursing and Midwifery Council (NMC) standards.

Key facts (verified against the NMC Standards of proficiency for registered nurses, 2024):

- UK pre-registration nursing requires a minimum of 4,600 programme hours: at least 2,300 theory and at least 2,300 supervised practice (placement) hours. Up to 600 of the practice hours may be simulated learning. PlaceMate tracks progress toward the 2,300-hour practice target.
- The NMC proficiencies are organised into 7 platforms plus Annexe A (communication skills) and Annexe B (11 nursing procedures). PlaceMate tracks all 219 individual proficiency statements.
- PlaceMate is student-first and starts with the adult field of nursing.
- PlaceMate is free for students, uses privacy-first cookieless analytics (no cookie banner), and is designed to hold no patient-identifiable data.
- PlaceMate is independent and not affiliated with or endorsed by the NMC.

The app lives at https://app.placemate.uk. The marketing site is https://placemate.uk.

## Pages

- [Home](https://placemate.uk/): what PlaceMate is and who it's for.
- [Features](https://placemate.uk/features): placement hours tracker, NMC proficiency tracker, clinical skills passport, weekly shift planner, medication notes & drug-calculation practice, guided Gibbs reflections, revision timetable, self-care check-ins.
- [Pricing](https://placemate.uk/pricing): free for student nurses.
- [FAQ](https://placemate.uk/faq): answers on placement hours, NMC proficiencies, the PAD (Practice Assessment Document), reflections, drug calculations, and privacy.
- [For universities](https://placemate.uk/for-universities): how PlaceMate supports nursing programmes and practice-placement teams.
- [About](https://placemate.uk/about): what PlaceMate is and its independence from the NMC.
- [Contact](https://placemate.uk/contact): hello@placemate.uk.

## Guides

Practical, sourced guides for UK student nurses (educational, not clinical advice):

- [Guides hub](https://placemate.uk/guides): all guides, grouped by topic.
- [Student nurse placement hours, explained](https://placemate.uk/guides/student-nurse-placement-hours-explained): the 2,300-hour practice requirement, the 600-hour simulation cap, what counts, and how to track it.
- [The NMC proficiencies and your PAD, explained](https://placemate.uk/guides/nmc-proficiencies-and-your-pad): the 7 platforms, Annexe A and B, how the Practice Assessment Document works, and how to evidence proficiencies.
- [How to write a nursing reflection (with a Gibbs example)](https://placemate.uk/guides/how-to-write-a-nursing-reflection): the six Gibbs stages, a worked example, and a reusable template.
- [Nursing drug calculations: formulas and worked examples](https://placemate.uk/guides/nursing-drug-calculations): the dose formula, infusion rates, unit conversions, and a practice plan.
- [How to track your placement hours](https://placemate.uk/guides/how-to-track-placement-hours): what to record per shift, paper vs spreadsheet vs app, and the weekly reconcile habit.
- [What counts as placement hours?](https://placemate.uk/guides/what-counts-as-placement-hours): what counts (nights, sim, spoke days) and what doesn't (travel, sickness, paid work).
- [The 7 NMC platforms explained](https://placemate.uk/guides/nmc-platforms-explained): each platform in plain English and what it looks like on placement.
- [How to evidence NMC proficiencies](https://placemate.uk/guides/how-to-evidence-nmc-proficiencies): what counts as evidence, the capture habit, and how to make sign-offs easy.
- [Gibbs reflective cycle: a full worked example](https://placemate.uk/guides/gibbs-reflective-cycle-example): a complete annotated six-stage reflection from a student placement.
- [Driscoll's model of reflection](https://placemate.uk/guides/driscolls-model-of-reflection): What? So what? Now what? — prompts, a worked example, and when to choose it over Gibbs.

## Tools

Free, no-login tools that run entirely in the browser (nothing saved or sent):

- [Tools hub](https://placemate.uk/tools): all free tools.
- [Placement hours calculator](https://placemate.uk/tools/placement-hours-calculator): work out how many of the 2,300 NMC practice hours remain, with the 600-hour simulation cap handled automatically.
- [Drug calculation practice](https://placemate.uk/tools/drug-calculation-practice): unlimited randomly generated dose, oral-liquid, infusion-rate and unit-conversion questions with instant feedback and worked answers (practice only).
